params.name: user_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: id
params.description: The unique identifier for the channel.

params.name: status
params.description: The status of the feed items.

params.name: source
params.description: The workflow key associated with the message in the feed.

params.name: tenant
params.description: The tenant associated with the feed items.

params.name: has_tenant
params.description: Whether the feed items have a tenant.

params.name: workflow_categories[]
params.description: The workflow categories of the feed items.

params.name: archived
params.description: The archived status of the feed items.

params.name: trigger_data
params.description: The trigger data of the feed items (as a JSON string).

params.name: locale
params.description: The locale to render the feed items in. Must be in the IETF 5646 format (e.g. `en-US`). When not provided, will default to the locale that the feed items were rendered in. Only available for enterprise plan customers using custom translations.

params.name: exclude
params.description: Comma-separated list of field paths to exclude from the response. Use dot notation for nested fields (e.g., `entries.archived_at`). Limited to 3 levels deep.

params.name: mode
params.description: The mode to render the feed items in. Can be `compact` or `rich`. Defaults to `rich`. When `mode` is `compact`, feed items will not have `activities` and `total_activities` fields; the `data` field will not include nested arrays and objects; and the `actors` field will only have up to one actor.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of items per page (defaults to 50).

params.name: inserted_at.gt
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted after the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.gte
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted after or on the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.lt
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted before the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.lte
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted before or on the given date.

items.docs:
Returns a paginated list of feed items for a user in reverse chronological order, including metadata about the feed. If the user has not yet been identified within Knock, an empty feed will be returned.

You can customize the response using [response filters](/integrations/in-app/knock#customizing-api-response-content) to exclude or only include specific properties on your resources.

**Notes:**
* When making this call from a client-side environment, use your publishable key along with a user to

info.name: Update category in user preference set

items.docs: Updates a single category in a specific user preference set. This operation is deprecated.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The id of the user to merge into.

items.docs: Merge two users together, where the user specified with the `from_user_id` param will be merged into the user specified by `user_id`.

Bulk sets the preferences for up to 1,000 users at a time. The preference set `:id` can be either `default` or a `tenant.id`. Learn more about [per-tenant preferences](/preferences/tenant-preferences). Note that this is a destructive operation and will replace any existing users' preferences with the preferences sent.
Identifies multiple users in a single operation. Allows creating or updating up to 1,000 users in a single batch with various properties, preferences, and channel data.
Updates the workflows in a specific user preference set. This operation is deprecated.

items.docs: Updates the categories in a specific user preference set. This operation is deprecated.
Permanently deletes up to 1,000 users at a time.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of items per page (defaults to 50).

params.name: tenant
params.description: Limits the results to items with the corresponding tenant.

params.name: channel_id
params.description: Limits the results to items with the corresponding channel ID.

params.name: status[]
params.description: Limits the results to messages with the given delivery status.

params.name: engagement_status[]
params.description: Limits the results to messages with the given engagement status.

params.name: message_ids[]
params.description: Limits the results to only the message IDs given (max 50). Note: when using this option, the results will be subject to any other filters applied to the query.

params.name: workflow_categories[]
params.description: Limits the results to messages related to any of the provided categories.

params.name: source
params.description: Limits the results to messages triggered by the given workflow key.

params.name: workflow_run_id
params.description: Limits the results to messages associated with the top-level workflow run ID returned by the workflow trigger request.

params.name: workflow_recipient_run_id
params.description: Limits the results to messages for a specific recipient's workflow run.

params.name: trigger_data
params.description: Limits the results to only messages that were generated with the given data. See [trigger data filtering](/api-reference/overview/trigger-data-filtering) for more information.

params.name: inserted_at.gt
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted after the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.gte
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted after or on the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.lt
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted before the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.lte
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted before or on the given date.

params.name: user_id
params.description: The user ID to list messages for.

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of messages for a specific user. Messages are sorted with the most recent ones appearing first. Messages outside the account's retention window will not be included in the results.
